title: Genetic Modification & Synthetic Biology
description: Questions about genetic engineering, synthetic biology, and de novo synthesis in your research.

questions.text: What capabilities are being engineered or what functions are being modified?
Do the modifications you are making overlap with any gain-of-function categories (enhanced transmissibility, virulence, host range, immune evasion, therapeutic resistance)?

signals.concernDescription: Your genetic modifications overlap with recognised gain-of-function categories. This requires the same dual-use consideration as direct gain-of-function work.
You were unsure whether your modifications overlap with gain-of-function categories. Consider reviewing the categories listed in Phase 3A.

questions.text: Does your work involve creating genetic sequences de novo — either by synthesising them yourself (e.g., benchtop device) or by ordering synthetic nucleic acids from a commercial provider (e.g., IDT, Twist Bioscience, GenScript)?

options.label: Yes, ordered from a commercial provider

signals.concernDescription: Your project involves de novo synthesis of genetic sequences. Commercial providers typically screen orders, but researchers retain responsibility for understanding dual-use implications.
Your project involves ordering synthetic sequences from a provider. Provider screening varies by company and jurisdiction.
Your project involves both in-house and provider-sourced synthetic sequences.

followUp.text: Could these sequences, or parts of them, encode proteins or functions associated with pathogenicity, toxicity, or immune evasion?

signals.concernDescription: Your synthetic sequences could encode pathogenicity or toxicity functions. This is a significant dual-use concern that requires careful oversight.

signals.concernTitle: Pathogenicity potential of synthetic sequences uncertain
signals.concernDescription: You were unsure whether your synthetic sequences could encode pathogenicity functions. Consider having a bioinformatics screen performed.

questions.text: Could your genetic constructs be transferred to, or function in, pathogenic organisms?

signals.concernDescription: Your genetic constructs could function in pathogenic organisms. This transferability is a dual-use consideration.
You were unsure whether your constructs could function in pathogenic organisms.
